purpose: Single promoted Area 01 source map for runtime v3. This makes the uploaded open-surface-over-seafloor-with-dive-holes plan the runtime geometry/collision contract. It merges the surface-floor source reference, Godot-coordinate geometry, explicit scene hooks, object placement metadata, terrain-kit references, and defect-prevention rules.

promotion_decision.current_runtime_replaced_when: Main.tscn calls the Godot builder, which creates visible terrain, collision, rims, and nonblocking debug hooks from this file during startup.
promotion_decision.old_blockout_policy: area_01_blockout_source_map_v1.json is historical fallback material only; runtime v3 is the current geometry/collision source.
promotion_decision.user_visible_acceptance: The full water surface must remain open horizontally. The first major solid boundary is the seafloor shelf below the surface. Exploration complexity lives beneath that shelf through authored holes, caves, pockets, gates, and return currents.

comparison_summary.runtime_blockout: Models a boat/moonpool surface-entry lane and central descent spine.
comparison_summary.accepted_surface_floor: Models an open horizontal surface-water band across the full stage plus a separate oxygen refill hook.
comparison_summary.reconciliation: The promoted map should use the accepted open-surface rule, while preserving ship/moonpool banking as a separate offload hook.

comparison_summary.runtime_blockout: Uses rescue-pass wall masses and water lanes to make current collision readable.
comparison_summary.accepted_surface_floor: Uses continuous seafloor shelf segments with cave holes and named cave systems below.
comparison_summary.reconciliation: Use accepted surface-floor terrain as the base for runtime v2; retire direct blockout wall topology after migration.

comparison_summary.runtime_blockout: Uses resource pockets, cave-mouth affordances, and validation rules.
comparison_summary.accepted_surface_floor: Uses explicit scene_hooks for oxygen, offload, cave entrances, hazards, gates, pickups, scans, and return currents.
comparison_summary.reconciliation: Promoted runtime source should use explicit scene_hooks and preserve relevant blockout defect-prevention validation rules.

comparison_summary.runtime_blockout: Contains existing visible_path, collision_path, and lip_path fields for current runtime builder nodes.
comparison_summary.accepted_surface_floor: Contains visual/collision/rim strategies but no current scene node paths because it is not runtime-wired.
comparison_summary.reconciliation: The next implementation pass needs a builder or conversion layer that creates visible terrain, collision, rim trims, and Area2D hooks from accepted geometry.

coordinate_space.origin: Area 01 planning world coordinates; y increases downward; surface water starts near y=0.
coordinate_space.conversion: Hand-authored from docs/planning/maps/area_01_surface_floor_source_map_v1.png topology into Godot-like world coordinates. Do not infer collision from source PNG pixels.
coordinate_space.runtime_authority: Current Area 01 geometry/collision authority. Main.tscn calls Area01BlockoutBuilder, which builds runtime terrain, collision, rims, and validation hooks from this file; this runtime v3 file is promoted from the uploaded surface-floor source map and its Godot-coordinate geometry.

pipeline.runtime_object_model: platform_objects plus interactive_scene_objects plus foreground_occluders plus scene_hooks

representation.solid_collision: StaticBody2D with CollisionPolygon2D child using the same source polygon
representation.terrain_trims: Sprite2D placements for rim strips, cave-mouth frames, props, gates, resources, and decor

topology.open_surface_rule: The full top of the level remains open water for oxygen recovery.
topology.banking_rule: Ship/moonpool offload is a separate hook from oxygen surface refill.
topology.seafloor_rule: A continuous seafloor shelf sits under open water, with visible cave mouths cut through it.
topology.route_rule: Route complexity lives below the seafloor through named cave regions with depth, hazard, gate, resource, and return-current support.

runtime_generation_contract.collision_source: solid_terrain polygon points and scene_hooks points
runtime_generation_contract.visual_source: terrain kit manifest plus generated props, not preview PNG pixels
runtime_generation_contract.rule: visible terrain polygon == collision polygon source == rim/trim placement source for every solid terrain entry

regions.role: Free horizontal surface band for oxygen recovery and choosing the next dive hole.
Readable terrain horizon below open ocean; cave mouths cut through it.
Small safe cave under the ship-side seafloor for first resources and fast turnaround.
First wider resource/banking route that teaches cargo return pressure.
Deeper vertical route with return-current support and higher-value materials.
Right-side medium branch with wreck promise and pressure gate.
Deepest locked exit that hints at later route expansion.

solid_terrain.role: Left continuous seafloor segment framing starter and shell routes.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Polygon2D reef fill plus top rim strip and sparse kelp/coral Sprite2D placements
solid_terrain.rim_strategy: Sprite2D/Polygon2D cyan lip along upper playable edge

solid_terrain.role: Seafloor lip left of the ship moonpool and starter hole.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Polygon2D reef fill plus moonpool-adjacent rim
solid_terrain.rim_strategy: Readable edge band around moonpool approach

solid_terrain.role: Seafloor lip right of the starter hole leading toward Shell Reef.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Polygon2D reef fill plus top rim strip
solid_terrain.rim_strategy: Cyan lip on playable top and cave-entry underside

solid_terrain.role: Central seafloor segment between Shell Reef and pressure routes.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Polygon2D reef fill plus sparse crystals and rim strips
solid_terrain.rim_strategy: Thin cyan lip on top/underside playable edges

solid_terrain.role: Right seafloor segment above pressure wreck branch.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Polygon2D reef fill plus top rim and right-branch mouth frame
solid_terrain.rim_strategy: Rim trims around the pressure wreck hole

solid_terrain.role: Left wall/floor of starter kelp hole.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Polygon2D cave wall fill with kelp decor Sprite2D placements
solid_terrain.rim_strategy: Cave-mouth trim where it borders playable water

solid_terrain.role: Terrain divider between starter hole and Shell Reef bank cave.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Polygon2D cave wall fill with strong entry rim
solid_terrain.rim_strategy: Rim strips along starter and Shell Reef openings

solid_terrain.role: Small navigational shelves inside Shell Reef bank cave.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Narrow ledge strip with edge trim; collision matches polygon
solid_terrain.rim_strategy: Top lip strip; no hidden blocker past visual edge

solid_terrain.role: Floor mass around thermal vent pocket.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Warm vent floor reef fill, vent props remain separate Sprite2D placements

solid_terrain.role: West wall framing deeper Blue Chimney descent.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Tall cave wall fill with vertical rim strips
solid_terrain.rim_strategy: Vertical cave rim toward playable descent

solid_terrain.role: Lower Blue Chimney floor and future exit approach.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Deep floor fill with darker underside and mineral Sprite2D placements

solid_terrain.role: Right branch wall enclosing the pressure wreck pocket.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Cave wall fill with wreck chunks and pressure gate as separate Sprite2D placements
solid_terrain.rim_strategy: Cave mouth and locked-route rim trims

solid_terrain.role: Interior ceiling mass that closes the pressure wreck branch into a cave-like pocket without blocking the gate or scan zone.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Cave ceiling fill with underside trim so the branch reads as enclosed terrain instead of open rectangle.
solid_terrain.rim_strategy: Underside and diagonal trims around the playable pocket ceiling

solid_terrain.role: Lower shelf mass under the pressure wreck pocket that frames the branch floor while leaving the pocket water navigable.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Cave floor fill with readable top lip and darker underside.
solid_terrain.rim_strategy: Top lip and deep-floor trim along the pocket floor

solid_terrain.role: Deep right ceiling mass above the future exit lane so the lower route reads as a corridor instead of blank open water.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Deep cave ceiling fill with subdued underside and diagonal trims.
solid_terrain.rim_strategy: Subdued underside trim above the future-exit playable lane

solid_terrain.role: Solid right-side cave endcap behind the locked future gate so the corridor does not read as open ocean.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Irregular right wall fill behind the future locked gate; the gate hook owns route blocking.
solid_terrain.rim_strategy: Vertical endcap trim on the playable corridor side

solid_terrain.role: Left pillar framing the future deep exit corridor while keeping the future exit trigger open.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Vertical cave pillar fill that narrows the lower-right lane without closing the entrance.
solid_terrain.rim_strategy: Vertical wall trims on the playable corridor side

solid_terrain.role: Lower-right future route floor around locked exit.
solid_terrain.visual_strategy: Quiet locked-route floor fill; locked gate stays separate

cave_mouths.readability_role: Visible first opening under the ship-side seafloor.
cave_mouths.collision_rule: No blocking collision across the center of this entrance.

cave_mouths.readability_role: Second hole into the wider Shell Reef banking route.
cave_mouths.collision_rule: No blocking collision across the entrance center.

cave_mouths.readability_role: Central drop into the vent pocket.
cave_mouths.collision_rule: Collision frames the drop; center remains navigable.

cave_mouths.readability_role: Deeper vertical route from Shell Reef toward Blue Chimney.
cave_mouths.collision_rule: No hidden wall across descent center.

cave_mouths.readability_role: Right-side opening toward pressure wreck branch.
cave_mouths.collision_rule: Gate collision belongs to the pressure gate hook, not the terrain hole.
Future lock is explicit gate hook, not inferred terrain collision.

object_layer.purpose: Object-layer companion data for the Area 01 side-scroll map pipeline. These entries describe editable Godot object intent; they do not grant gameplay behavior until wired by an implementation pass.

parallax_layers.role: Surface sky/waterline band above the ocean.
Distant non-colliding reef shelves and towers.
Soft underwater light shafts and depth haze.
Non-colliding near-background rock mass behind foreground terrain.

object_layers.role: Player-blocking collision matching terrain source polygons.
Ship, decor, resources, wreck pieces, gates, and trim sprites.
Oxygen, offload, cave entrances, pickups, scans, hazards, pressure gates, return currents, and route triggers.

asset_families.recommended_strategy: Polygon2D fill with optional texture; do not use square prop packs.
one_by_one or custom_wide_pack; must align to cave mouth polygons.
prop_pack_3x3 after terrain geometry is accepted.
prop_pack_3x3 with transparent output and separate pickup Area2D hooks.
one_by_one; explicit Area2D gate hook owns behavior.

implementation_notes: Do not cut collision from the source PNG or preview PNG.
Do not use stage-reference art as runtime background.
Do not attach resource, scan, pressure, predator, oxygen, or progression logic to Sprite2D nodes.
Runtime promotion should generate or edit Godot scene nodes from the source geometry JSON.

camera_bounds.notes: Use camera-scale previews to verify wall readability before promotion to Main.tscn.

validation_rules.rule: Every enabled CollisionPolygon2D blocker in Area01ArtSlice/TerrainCollision must have a mapped visible Polygon2D solid and a mapped Polygon2D rim/lip.
Any opaque terrain-looking Polygon2D must either be mapped as solid terrain or listed as nonblocking background/decor.
Playable water lanes are planned as open movement spaces; collision should frame their edges, not cross their centers.
Current pickups, scan targets, and interactables must be reachable from a playable lane unless intentionally marked as future-locked.
Background, lighting, parallax, and dressing shapes may not own player-blocking collision.

Foreground playable terrain must use the source-map terrain chunk library: filled terrain polygons, simplified matching collision, continuous edge treatment, and no rectangular sprite stamping as the primary wall read.
Scannable targets must be reachable and nonblocking in the current run, or visually quiet and explicitly locked as future content.
Starter pockets should be spread across left, right, and deeper lanes so resources teach the room layout instead of clustering in one tiny area.
Cave mouths should distinguish openings from solid terrain without adding exact arrows, checklist markers, minimap behavior, or label-only comprehension.
Open surface spans the full top of the map and is not blocked by terrain collision.
Ship offload zone is separate from oxygen refill zone.
Every solid terrain entry has Polygon2D visual, CollisionPolygon2D collision, and rim/lip strategy.
Every cave mouth has a matching cave_entrance scene hook and no blocker across the entrance center.
Current pickup and scan zones sit inside reachable planned regions.
Future locked gates are explicit Area2D/interactable hooks and must not be inferred from pixels.
Parallax backgrounds, lights, plants, crystals, and background rock do not own player-blocking collision.
This file must be promoted intentionally before it replaces area_01_blockout_source_map_v1.json as runtime authority.
Runtime v3 terrain visuals, collision polygons, rim trims, interaction hooks, debug overlays, and previews must derive from this surface-floor source map.
Preview PNGs are QA artifacts only and must not be used as the runtime map.
The top water surface must remain open across the full stage for repeated oxygen recovery during the day run.
The ship/moonpool offload hook must stay separate from the broad surface oxygen refill band.
Resource pickups, fish, scannables, gates, hazards, and route hooks must be placed in playable water regions, not inside solid terrain polygons.

migration_plan.task: Generate preview from this file and verify it matches the uploaded open-surface, seafloor, cave-hole direction.
Build or update the Godot Area 01 builder to create terrain Polygon2D, StaticBody2D/CollisionPolygon2D, rim/trim Sprite2D, and Area2D hooks from this file.
Run collision/debug overlay validation that proves visible terrain, collision, and rim trims share source polygons.
Mark this file as runtime authority and demote the old blockout map to historical fallback material.

non_goals: No baked preview PNG runtime map.
No new route content, creatures, upgrades, resources, or gameplay promises in this source-map promotion.
No collision inferred from sprite alpha or generated pixels.
No final production art guarantee; v4 remains a prototype terrain-art direction.
